content.text: Early in the morning, a tiny brown rabbit hopped out of its warm hole. It ran quickly under the tall green bushes and searched for fresh grass. Near the clear blue stream, it stopped to drink some water before running back home under the trees.

title: Helping Dad in the Yard
hint: Common action verbs, time words, prepositions.

content.text: On Saturday afternoon, my dad and I worked outside in the backyard. First, we gathered the dry leaves with a big yellow rake. Then, we put them into large bags. Finally, we watered the beautiful flowers together and sat down for cold juice.

content.text: Billy could not find his favorite toy train anywhere in the house. He looked behind the blue couch, under the soft rug, and inside the big toy box. Suddenly, he saw the shiny red train peeking out from behind his bedroom door. He smiled happily.

title: A Hungry Bird's Search
hint: Check prepositions (down, from, across, near, back to) and verbs.

content.text: A little robin flew down from the branch of an oak tree. It walked slowly across the damp green grass and looked for dinner. Soon, it spotted a long worm near the garden fence. The bird pulled it out and flew back to its nest.

title: Baking Bread with Grandma
hint: Sequence words (today, first, next) and common action verbs.

content.text: Today, Grandma taught me how to bake fresh white bread in the kitchen. First, we measured the flour and water into a large bowl. Next, we mixed the dough with our hands. We put the bread into the warm oven and waited patiently for it to cook.

questions.question: What is this passage mostly about?

title: The Playful Squirrel's Mistake
hint: Descriptive adverbs, compound words, and dialogue.

content.text: A playful squirrel scurried quickly up the tall pine tree, holding a small brown acorn in its mouth. It decided to hide the acorn inside a dark hole, but the hole was already full of dry leaves. "Oh no!" squeaked the squirrel. It dropped the acorn onto the soft grass below and searched for another tree. Finally, it found a safe branch.

The little kitten purred happily and fell asleep.

content.text: During our weekend walk in the deep woods, my brother and I discovered a hidden pathway. The path was covered with yellow wildflowers and green moss. It was a wonderful adventure.

questions.question: What is this passage mostly about?

title: The Mystery of the Missing Keys
hint: Prefixes (un-, re-, mis-), multi-syllable words, and tricky vowels.

content.text: My mother was unhappy because she misplaced her car keys again. We started an untidy search through the entire living room. First, we checked under the sofa cushions, but they were not there. Then, we decided to rebuild our search area and looked in the kitchen. Unexpectedly, my little sister found the keys inside the refrigerator next to the milk. Mother smiled and thanked her, and we promised to always return them to the hook by the doorway.

title: Comprehension: The Mystery of the Missing Keys

content.passage: My mother was unhappy because she misplaced her car keys again. We started an untidy search through the entire living room. First, we checked under the sofa cushions, but they were not there. Then, we decided to rebuild our search area and looked in the kitchen. Unexpectedly, my little sister found the keys inside the refrigerator next to the milk. Mother smiled and thanked her, and we promised to always return them to the hook by the doorway.

questions.question: What is this passage mostly about?

content.text: A tiny sea turtle hatched from its egg on the sandy beach. It felt unsafe because the ocean waves were loud, but it crawled bravely toward the water. The little turtle had to navigate around deep holes and drift wood. Suddenly, a gentle wave swept it into the cool ocean. It swam excitedly into the deep water to begin its new life. Many colorful fish swam beside it, and the young turtle felt happy and free at last.

title: Comprehension: The Brave Little Sea Turtle

questions.question: What is this passage mostly about?

title: Exploring the Starry Night Sky
hint: Prefixes (unusually, rewriting, unforgettable), multi-syllable, tricky vowels.

content.text: Last night, the sky was unusually clear, so we set up our telescope in the backyard. We wanted to observe the bright moon and search for distant planets. My father pointed to a group of stars and explained how they formed patterns. "That is the Big Dipper!" he said excitedly. We watched the stars twinkle for an hour before rewriting our observations in our science notebooks. It was an unforgettable night, and I want to study astronomy.

title: Comprehension: Exploring the Starry Night Sky

content.passage: Last night, the sky was unusually clear, so we set up our telescope in the backyard. We wanted to observe the bright moon and search for distant planets. My father pointed to a group of stars and explained how they formed patterns. "That is the Big Dipper!" he said excitedly. We watched the stars twinkle for an hour before rewriting our observations in our science notebooks. It was an unforgettable night, and I want to study astronomy.

questions.question: What is this passage mostly about?

content.text: During a hot summer afternoon, a thirsty crow searched frantically for water. It discovered a glass pitcher on a garden table, but the water level was too low to reach. The crow realized it was unable to tip the heavy pitcher over. It decided to reuse some small pebbles from the garden path. The crow dropped them one by one into the glass. Slowly, the water rose, and the clever bird drank happily.

title: Comprehension: A Clever Crow's Big Idea

The crow realized it was unable to tip the heavy pitcher over. It decided to reuse some small pebbles from the garden path. The crow dropped them one by one into the glass. Slowly, the water rose, and the clever bird drank happily.

questions.question: What is this passage mostly about?

title: A Special School Science Project
hint: Prefixes (recycle, reuse, repaint), multi-syllable, tricky vowels.

content.text: Our class was excited to prepare a science project about recycling. We wanted to show how to reuse old plastic bottles to make beautiful flower pots. First, we gathered thirty empty bottles from the school cafeteria. Then, we repainted them with bright colors and filled them with rich soil. Finally, we planted green seeds and watched them sprout. Our teacher praised our hard work, and we felt proud of our green classroom.

title: Comprehension: A Special School Science Project

Our teacher praised our hard work, and we felt proud of our green classroom.
